description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle and a solid, dark metal head. The handle is ergonomically shaped, widening at the base for a secure grip and tapering toward the head, with visible wood grain texture. The axe head is rectangular with a single, straight cutting edge and a flat poll on the opposite side, suitable for both chopping and hammering tasks. The blade is mounted perpendicularly to the handle, with the metal head securely fitted onto the wood, likely using a traditional wedged method. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements or branding. This tool is commonly found in urban garages, workshops, or maintenance areas for cutting wood or demolition tasks.
description: A sturdy hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a rectangular, dark metal head featuring a straight cutting edge and flat poll. The design is practical and robust, suitable for chopping or hammering.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the wooden handle and the narrow profile of the axe head.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the handle and the rectangular shape of the axe head with its straight edge.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the thickness of the handle and the width of the axe head, including the cutting edge.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the handle's curvature and the flat poll of the axe head.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with ergonomic grip, oval cross-section, and flared base for secure handling.
attribute_blade_design: Rectangular steel blade with a single straight cutting edge and a flat poll; no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted onto the handle.
